On Feb. 23, 2026:
Subject: Thank you for your request. (Autoreply)
Thank you for contacting the Judicial Council of California's Public Access to Judicial Administrative Records (PAJAR) team. This message is an automatic reply to let you know that we received your request.

Please read the message below because it has information that may help you.

*** PLEASE DO NOT REPLY to this e-mail message. Responses to this automatic reply are not monitored.***

WHAT TO EXPECT

* If you are asking for court case records-for example, a court order, divorce records, name change records, criminal history records- this message will be the only response you receive. Please read the section below, The PAJAR Team Cannot Provide These Records, to find information that may be helpful.

* If you are asking for judicial administrative records from the Judicial Council, the Supreme Court of California, or a California Court of Appeal, the PAJAR team will send you a response within about 10 days.

* Here are some examples of judicial administrative records: signed contracts, final audit reports, budget and spending reports, reports to the Legislature, and procurement records. You can find more information about judicial administrative records at courts.ca.gov/cms/rules/index/ten/rule10_500<https://courts.ca.gov/cms/rules/index/ten/rule10_500> and at courts.ca.gov/policy-administration/public-records<https://courts.ca.gov/policy-administration/public-records>.

* The PAJAR team may ask you for more information to be sure it understands your request. The PAJAR team may also notify you if it needs more time to complete a search.

* When work on your request is complete, the PAJAR team will notify you whether there are disclosable responsive records or not. If there are disclosable responsive copies, the PAJAR team will provide electronic copies, if possible.

THE PAJAR TEAM CANNOT PROVIDE THESE RECORDS

> Court Case Records-Superior Courts of California

The PAJAR team cannot provide copies of superior court case records. If you want records filed in a specific superior court case, you must contact the court directly. California has 58 superior courts, one in each county. You can find links to each court's website at courts.ca.gov/find-my-court<https://courts.ca.gov/find-my-court>. Each superior court clerk is responsible for that court's records.

Some superior court websites allow the public to search and view some types of case records online. If a court's website does not allow you to search and view case records online, you must call or visit the court clerk's office for help. Superior courts post their telephone numbers and street addresses on their websites. Some also post e-mail addresses.

Examples of records that you must request by contacting a superior court:

* Divorce decrees;

* Traffic citations;

* Name change records;

* Wills;

* Criminal case records (for example, orders imposing sentences and judgments); and

* Restraining orders.

You will find more information about some of these topics in the message below.

> Court Case Records-Supreme Court of California and California Courts of Appeal

The PAJAR team cannot provide copies of appellate court case records. You can find information about specific cases filed with the Supreme Court of California or with the California Courts of Appeal at appellatecases.courtinfo.ca.gov<http://appellatecases.courtinfo.ca.gov/>.

You can review opinions that the Supreme Court of California and the California Courts of Appeal issued in specific cases at courts.ca.gov/opinions.htm<http://www.courts.ca.gov/opinions.htm>.

You can find the following Supreme Court of California case records online:

* Legal briefs filed in cases argued before the court: supreme.courts.ca.gov/case-information/briefs-argued-cases<https://supreme.courts.ca.gov/case-information/briefs-argued-cases>; and

* Official minutes going back three years: supreme.courts.ca.gov/case-information/minutes<https://supreme.courts.ca.gov/case-information/minutes>.

To view or request copies of records filed in a specific case before the Supreme Court of California, you must call or visit the court. Supreme Court contact information is posted at supreme.courts.ca.gov/about-court/court-locations-and-contacts<https://supreme.courts.ca.gov/about-court/court-locations-and-contacts>.

To view or request copies of records filed in specific cases before a California Court of Appeal you must call or visit the court. You can find a list of the six Courts of Appeal at appellate.courts.ca.gov<https://appellate.courts.ca.gov/>. On the left side of that page, select the court where the case is filed. Then scroll down to select "Contact Us." On that page you will find court telephone numbers and location addresses.

If you have a Court of Appeal case number, but you do not know the court where the case is filed, please visit appellate.courts.ca.gov<https://appellate.courts.ca.gov/>. That page lists the letters that begin the case numbers for each of the Courts of Appeal.

Information about California appellate court cases also is available at your local law library, which may provide free public access to computer-assisted legal research systems such as LexisNexis or Westlaw. To learn more about local public law libraries, and to locate one near you, visit selfhelp.courts.ca.gov/court-basics/law-libraries<https://selfhelp.courts.ca.gov/court-basics/law-libraries>.

> Court Case Records-United States Federal Courts

The PAJAR team cannot provide copies of records filed in cases before federal courts located in California. To find information about those federal courts-including court telephone numbers and location addresses for those courts-please visit:

* www.cand.uscourts.gov<http://www.cand.uscourts.gov> (district court, Northern California);
* www.casd.uscourts.gov<http://www.casd.uscourts.gov> (district court, Southern California);
* www.caed.uscourts.gov<http://www.caed.uscourts.gov> (district court, Eastern California);
* www.cacd.uscourts.gov<http://www.cacd.uscourts.gov> (district court, Central California); and
* www.ca9.uscourts.gov<http://www.ca9.uscourts.gov> (Courts of Appeal).

> Birth or Death Certificates

The PAJAR team cannot provide copies of birth or death certificates. The registrar or recorder in the county where the birth or death occurred would have these records.

You can find a list of all California county registrars and recorders, and their contact information, at www.cdph.ca.gov/Programs/CHSI/Pages/County-Registrars-and-Recorders.aspx<http://www.cdph.ca.gov/Programs/CHSI/Pages/County-Registrars-and-Recorders.aspx<http://www.cdph.ca.gov/Programs/CHSI/Pages/County-Registrars-and-Recorders.aspx%3chttp:/www.cdph.ca.gov/Programs/CHSI/Pages/County-Registrars-and-Recorders.aspx<http://www.cdph.ca.gov/Programs/CHSI/Pages/County-Registrars-and-Recorders.aspx%3chttp:/www.cdph.ca.gov/Programs/CHSI/Pages/County-Registrars-and-Recorders.aspx%3chttp:/www.cdph.ca.gov/Programs/CHSI/Pages/County-Registrars-and-Recorders.aspx%3chttp:/www.cdph.ca.gov/Programs/CHSI/Pages/County-Registrars-and-Recorders.aspx>>>.

For information about how to request a birth or death certificate, please visit the website of California's Department of Public Health, at www.cdph.ca.gov/Programs/CHSI/Pages/Vital-Records.aspx<http://www.cdph.ca.gov/Programs/CHSI/Pages/Vital-Records.aspx<http://www.cdph.ca.gov/Programs/CHSI/Pages/Vital-Records.aspx%3chttp:/www.cdph.ca.gov/Programs/CHSI/Pages/Vital-Records.aspx<http://www.cdph.ca.gov/Programs/CHSI/Pages/Vital-Records.aspx%3chttp:/www.cdph.ca.gov/Programs/CHSI/Pages/Vital-Records.aspx%3chttp:/www.cdph.ca.gov/Programs/CHSI/Pages/Vital-Records.aspx%3chttp:/www.cdph.ca.gov/Programs/CHSI/Pages/Vital-Records.aspx>>>.

> Domestic Violence Information

The PAJAR team cannot provide copies of restraining or protective orders. You can find Information about where and how to ask for help in domestic violence circumstances on the California Courts Self-Help website at selfhelp.courts.ca.gov/DV-restraining-order<https://selfhelp.courts.ca.gov/DV-restraining-order>.

If you want copies of a restraining or protective order, you must contact the superior court where the case is filed. You can find links to all 58 superior court websites at courts.ca.gov/find-my-court<https://courts.ca.gov/find-my-court>. Court websites list court telephone numbers and locations.

> Criminal Background Records

The PAJAR team cannot provide copies of criminal history records. You must request copies from the superior court where the criminal case is filed. You can find links to all 58 superior court websites at courts.ca.gov/find-my-court<https://courts.ca.gov/find-my-court>. Court websites list court telephone numbers and locations.

Federal, state, and local law enforcement agencies are responsible for investigating criminal activity. If you suspect criminal activity is taking place, please contact law enforcement authorities. You can find a list of California Law Enforcement Agencies at post.ca.gov/le-agencies<https://post.ca.gov/le-agencies>.

> Name Change Records

The PAJAR team cannot provide copies of name change records. The California State Archives stores some name change records. Please visit the State Archives website, at www.sos.ca.gov/archives/about-archives/contact<http://www.sos.ca.gov/archives/about-archives/contact/<http://www.sos.ca.gov/archives/about-archives/contact%3chttp:/www.sos.ca.gov/archives/about-archives/contact/<http://www.sos.ca.gov/archives/about-archives/contact%3chttp:/www.sos.ca.gov/archives/about-archives/contact/%3chttp:/www.sos.ca.gov/archives/about-archives/contact%3chttp:/www.sos.ca.gov/archives/about-archives/contact/>>> to learn more. You can also call the California State Archives reference desk at 916-653-2246.

Information about the process for completing a name change can be found on the California Courts Self-Help website at selfhelp.courts.ca.gov/name-change<https://selfhelp.courts.ca.gov/name-change>.

> Traffic Citations

The PAJAR team cannot provide copies of traffic citations. Each superior court handles traffic matters independently and is responsible for its own records. If you have questions about a traffic ticket, please contact the superior court listed on the ticket (i.e., the "Notice to Appear"). You can find links to all 58 superior court websites at courts.ca.gov/find-my-court<https://courts.ca.gov/find-my-court>. Court websites list court telephone numbers and locations.

You can find general information about traffic tickets on the California Courts Self-Help website at selfhelp.courts.ca.gov/traffic<https://selfhelp.courts.ca.gov/traffic>.

> Wills

The PAJAR team cannot provide copies of a will for someone who has died. If a copy of the will has been filed or lodged with a superior court, you must contact the superior court to request a copy. You can find links to all 58 superior court websites at courts.ca.gov/find-my-court<https://courts.ca.gov/find-my-court>. Court websites list court telephone numbers and locations.

For general information about wills, estates, and probate matters, please visit the California Courts Self-Help website at selfhelp.courts.ca.gov/wills-estates-probate<https://selfhelp.courts.ca.gov/wills-estates-probate>.

> Jury Duty Information

The PAJAR team cannot answer questions about jury duty. If you have questions about a juror summons, or want to be excused from jury duty, please contact the superior court that issued the jury summons. You can find links to all 58 superior court websites at courts.ca.gov/find-my-court<https://courts.ca.gov/find-my-court>. This page also contains links to the pages on each court's website that has information about juror service. Court websites list court telephone numbers and locations.

You can find general information about jury service in California by visiting courts.ca.gov/courts/jury-service<https://courts.ca.gov/courts/jury-service>.

We hope this information is helpful. Thank you for your interest in the California judicial branch.

Sincerely,
Public Access to Judicial Administrative Records
Legal Services | Leadership Services Division
Judicial Council of California
455 Golden Gate Avenue
San Francisco, California 94102-3688
415-865-7796 | PAJAR@jud.ca.gov<mailto:PAJAR@jud.ca.gov<mailto:PAJAR@jud.ca.gov%3cmailto:PAJAR@jud.ca.gov<mailto:PAJAR@jud.ca.gov%3cmailto:PAJAR@jud.ca.gov%3cmailto:PAJAR@jud.ca.gov%3cmailto:PAJAR@jud.ca.gov>>> | courts.ca.gov/policy-administration/public-records<https://courts.ca.gov/policy-administration/public-records>

The PAJAR team cannot answer questions about jury duty. If you have questions about a juror summons, or want to be excused from jury duty, please contact the superior court that issued the jury summons. You can find links to all 58 superior court websites at courts.ca.gov/find-my-court<https://courts.ca.gov/find-my-court>. This page also contains links to the pages on each court's website that has information about juror service. Court websites list court telephone numbers and locations.

You can find general information about jury service in California by visiting courts.ca.gov/courts/jury-service<https://courts.ca.gov/courts/jury-service>.

We hope this information is helpful. Thank you for your interest in the California judicial branch.

Sincerely,

455 Golden Gate Avenue
San Francisco, California 94102-3688

On Feb. 8, 2026:
Subject: California Public Records Act Request: California official reports RFP, agreements, correspondence (Judicial Council of California)
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

The below requests are all in connection with the California Supreme Court's Request for Proposal RFP-CAL-2023-01-RP for publication of the California official reports (the "RFP"), made under Government Code section 68903, and which is available at https://courts.ca.gov/request/rfp-cal-2023-01-rp/rfp/publication-california-official-reports.

Government Code section 68903 states:

"The official reports shall be published under a contract to be entered into on behalf of the state by the Chief Justice of California, the Secretary of State, the Attorney General, the President of the State Bar, and the Reporter of Decisions, who shall serve as secretary."

REQUESTS:

1. All signed agreements made under proposals received pursuant to the RFP, including subsequent signed amendments (collectively, the "Agreements").
2. All correspondence with LexisNexis about the RFP.
3. All correspondence with LexisNexis about the Agreements.
4. All proposals received in response to the RFP.
5. All internal correspondence related to the RFP.

If assessing any confidentiality concerns, please note the following language in the RFP:

11.0 CONFIDENTIAL OR PROPRIETARY INFORMATION
PROPOSALS ARE SUBJECT TO DISCLOSURE TO THIRD PARTIES AND MEMBERS OF THE PUBLIC PURSUANT TO APPLICABLE LAWS, INCLUDING PUBLIC DISCLOSURE PURSUANT TO RULE 10.500 OF THE CALIFORNIA RULES OF COURT. Except as required by law, neither the Judicial Council nor the Court will disclose (i) social security numbers, or (ii) balance sheets or income statements submitted by a Publisher that is not a publicly traded corporation. All other information in Proposals may be disclosed in response to applicable public records requests, or as otherwise required by law. Such disclosure may be made regardless of whether the Proposal (or portions thereof) is marked "confidential," "proprietary," "copyright (c)," or otherwise, and regardless of any statement in the Proposal (a) purporting to limit the Judicial Council's or Court's right to disclose information in the Proposal, or (b) requiring the Judicial Council or Court to inform or obtain the consent of the Publisher prior to the disclosure of the Proposal (or portions thereof). Any Proposal that is password protected, or contains portions that are password protected, may be rejected. Submission of any Proposal pursuant to this RFP constitutes acknowledgment and consent by the Publisher to the potential public disclosure of its Proposal content, as set forth in this section. Publishers are accordingly cautioned not to include confidential, proprietary, or privileged information in proposals.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available, or by USB drive, CD-ROM, or online file sharing system if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Ansel Halliburton

On Feb. 8, 2026:
Subject: Thank you for your request. (Autoreply)
Thank you for contacting the Judicial Council of California's Public Access to Judicial Administrative Records (PAJAR) team. This message is an automatic reply to let you know that we received your request.

Please read the message below because it has information that may help you.

*** PLEASE DO NOT REPLY to this e-mail message. Responses to this automatic reply are not monitored.***

WHAT TO EXPECT

* If you are asking for court case records-for example, a court order, divorce records, name change records, criminal history records- this message will be the only response you receive. Please read the section below, The PAJAR Team Cannot Provide These Records, to find information that may be helpful.

* If you are asking for judicial administrative records from the Judicial Council, the Supreme Court of California, or a California Court of Appeal, the PAJAR team will send you a response within about 10 days.

* Here are some examples of judicial administrative records: signed contracts, final audit reports, budget and spending reports, reports to the Legislature, and procurement records. You can find more information about judicial administrative records at courts.ca.gov/cms/rules/index/ten/rule10_500<https://courts.ca.gov/cms/rules/index/ten/rule10_500> and at courts.ca.gov/policy-administration/public-records<https://courts.ca.gov/policy-administration/public-records>.

* The PAJAR team may ask you for more information to be sure it understands your request. The PAJAR team may also notify you if it needs more time to complete a search.

* When work on your request is complete, the PAJAR team will notify you whether there are disclosable responsive records or not. If there are disclosable responsive copies, the PAJAR team will provide electronic copies, if possible.

THE PAJAR TEAM CANNOT PROVIDE THESE RECORDS

> Court Case Records-Superior Courts of California

The PAJAR team cannot provide copies of superior court case records. If you want records filed in a specific superior court case, you must contact the court directly. California has 58 superior courts, one in each county. You can find links to each court's website at courts.ca.gov/find-my-court<https://courts.ca.gov/find-my-court>. Each superior court clerk is responsible for that court's records.

Some superior court websites allow the public to search and view some types of case records online. If a court's website does not allow you to search and view case records online, you must call or visit the court clerk's office for help. Superior courts post their telephone numbers and street addresses on their websites. Some also post e-mail addresses.

Examples of records that you must request by contacting a superior court:

* Divorce decrees;

* Traffic citations;

* Name change records;

* Wills;

* Criminal case records (for example, orders imposing sentences and judgments); and

* Restraining orders.

You will find more information about some of these topics in the message below.

> Court Case Records-Supreme Court of California and California Courts of Appeal

The PAJAR team cannot provide copies of appellate court case records. You can find information about specific cases filed with the Supreme Court of California or with the California Courts of Appeal at appellatecases.courtinfo.ca.gov<http://appellatecases.courtinfo.ca.gov/>.

You can review opinions that the Supreme Court of California and the California Courts of Appeal issued in specific cases at courts.ca.gov/opinions.htm<http://www.courts.ca.gov/opinions.htm>.

You can find the following Supreme Court of California case records online:

communications.subject: CPRA. Doogan. 03.11.2026
communications.communication:
Dear Ms. Doogan,

I write on behalf of the County of Santa Clara's Office of the District Attorney ("DA's Office") in response to your March 11, 2026 Public Records Act (CPRA) request.  Please accept our apologies for the delay in processing the request.

Attached are records responsive to your request for "all MOUs, contracts, agreements, audits, reports including financial documents for all partnerships with human trafficking task forces including all training materials for the time period of March 1, 2022 through February 28, 2026."


After a reasonable and diligent search, the DA's Office has determined that there are no responsive records to your request for "all MOUs, contracts, agreements, audits, reports including financial documents for all prostitution diversion programs for the time period of March 1, 2022 through February 28, 2026 prostitution diversion programs for the time period of March 1, 2022 through February 28, 2026."


With respect to your request for all "docket numbers for all California Penal Code Section 647(b), 653.25, 236.1, 266h and 266i for the time period of March 1, 2022 through February 28, 2026. Please provide all segregable related data, including the case number, the date and time on which the arrest or citation occurred, the officers involved, gender of those sited, DOB, race, name and the location of the occurrence",  the records that you request are exempt from disclosure under the CPRA as protected criminal offender record information and the right to privacy under the California Constitution and the Penal Code prohibits releasing the information that you are requesting. See Cal. Const. art. I, § 1; Gov. Code § 7927.705; Penal Code §§ 13102, 13201, 13300, 13303, 13304, 13305; the investigatory records exemption, Gov. Code, §§ 7923.600, 7923.605); and the official information privilege, Gov. Code § 7927.705,Evid. Code § 1040.

This concludes the response to your request.  Thank you.


[A picture containing circle, emblem, symbol, logo  Description automatically generated]
Stephanie M. Jackson | Deputy County Counsel
Office of the County Counsel, County of Santa Clara
70 West Hedding Street, East Wing, 9th Floor | San José, CA 95110
Office: (408) 299-5930 |  Mobile: (669) 786-6003
Stephanie.Jackson@cco.sccgov.org<mailto:Stephanie.Jackson@cco.sccgov.org> | counsel.sccgov.org<https://counsel.sccgov.org/>

NOTICE TO RECIPIENT: The information in this email is confidential and may be protected by the attorney-client and/or work product privileges. If you received this email in error, any review, use, dissemination, distribution, or copying of it is strictly prohibited.  Please notify Administration, Office of the County Counsel, of the error immediately at 408-299-5900 and delete this communication and any attached documents from your system.

communications.subject: The Missouri Sunshine Law Request: Missouri Overdrive (Office of the Attorney General - Missouri)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the The Missouri Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records:

Public reporting shows that, on two occasions this year, Missouri Secretary of State Denny Hoskins suspended funding to digital library provider OverDrive after allegations made by a state senator in March that minors could access inappropriate materials through the service. 

https://www.news-leader.com/story/news/state/2025/03/11/missouri-pulls-overdrive-funding-inappropriate-library-materials/82231654007/

https://www.msn.com/en-us/news/us/missouri-secretary-of-state-suspends-funds-for-ebook-app/ar-AA1AFpj4?apiversion=v2&noservercache=1&domshim=1&renderwebcomponents=1&wcseo=1&batchservertelemetry=1&noservertelemetry=1 

Pursuant to Missouri’s Sunshine Law and Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

1) Any messages, emails, memos, or other communications to, from, or including Secretary of State Denny Hoskins or State Sen. Mary Elizabeth Coleman with the words or "sexually explicit" or "pornographic" or “pornography” or “OverDrive” or “Sora” or “Libby” or “library” from January 1, 2025 until the time this request is processed.

2)  Any messages, emails, memos, presentations, or guidance about "OverDrive" or "sexually explicit" or "pornographic" content from January 1, 2025 until the time this request is processed.

3) Any emails to, from, or mentioning “OverDrive” from January 1, 2025 until the time this request is processed.

4) Any and all complaints filed related to the provision of resources by OverDrive from March 1, 2024 until the time this request is processed.

5) Any memos or communications sent to schools, superintendents, administrators, teachers, parents, students, other government organizations about library vendors from January 1, 2025 until the time this request is processed.

To help limit the extent of the responsive documents, please limit search from January 1, 2025 through the time this request is processed. Please deliver these documents on a rolling basis as responsive documents are found.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jason Koebler

title: NV CVR Data Request (2022, 2024) (White Pine County Clerk/Registrar of Voters)

communications.subject: Nevada Public Records Act Request: NV CVR Data Request (2022, 2024) (White Pine County Clerk/Registrar of Voters)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Nevada Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Cast vote record (CVR) data for the following elections: 
1. 2022 Nov Midterm Election 
2. 2024 Nov General Election

Specifically, for each of these elections, I’m looking for the full anonymized record of votes cast generated by the relevant voting system technology in White Pine County in that year.

In case it is helpful, you can find an example of the records I am requesting on the Clark County webpage, where they have made these records publicly available for the 2024 General Election. You can see this by navigating to their Election Information page, then clicking to the “Reports, Data, Maps” Section. If you scroll down, you will see “2024 Election Information” and then an itemized list that includes the item “Cast Vote Record for the Nov 5, 2024, General Election”, which is a link that downloads the data directly. I paste the link to the webpage here: https://www.clarkcountynv.gov/government/departments/elections/reports_data_maps/

Please let me know if you need any further clarification on the type of data that I am requesting.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Aleksandra Conevska

communications.subject: RE: Nevada Public Records Act Request: NV CVR Data Request (2022, 2024) (White Pine County Clerk/Registrar of Voters)
communications.communication:
They are attached. Thank you

title: ICE Detainers and 287(g) Policies (Cook County Sheriff)

communications.subject: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request: ICE Detainers and 287(g) Policies (Cook County Sheriff)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records:

Data from January 1, 2023 to the present showing:
1. The number of ICE detainer requests received
2. The number of detainers honored
3. The number of detainers declined
4. The number of individuals transferred to ICE custody
5. Current written policies, procedures, or guidance related to:
-ICE detainers
-Notification to ICE of release dates
-Participation in the 287(g) program (if applicable)
6. Emails sent or received by the Sheriff, Undersheriff, or Chief Deputies between January 1, 2023 and the present containing the term:
“287(g)”

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jasmine Lewin

title: West Los Angeles VA (West Los Angeles VA Medical Center)

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: West Los Angeles VA (West Los Angeles VA Medical Center)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

I request copies of all records that identify, describe, reference, define, govern, authorize, or discuss the use, occupancy, or control of federally owned land at the West Los Angeles VA Soldiers Home by Brentwood School.

For clarity and avoidance of doubt, the land referenced in this request is federal land that is part of the West Los Angeles VA Soldiers Home footprint, subject to deed restrictions requiring the property be used as a permanent home for disabled and homeless veterans.

This request includes all records concerning Brentwood School’s past or present use of any portion of this federally restricted land, regardless of how the land or facilities are described in the records.

The records requested include, but are not limited to:
	•	Leases, licenses, use agreements, permits, or memoranda of understanding involving Brentwood School
	•	Amendments, renewals, extensions, terminations, or enforcement actions related to such agreements
	•	Communications between the Department of Veterans Affairs and Brentwood School, including emails, letters, meeting notes, and attachments
	•	Records describing athletic fields, parking areas, buildings, access rights, maintenance responsibilities, or exclusive use
	•	Records addressing consideration, compensation, or in-kind benefits provided in exchange for land use
	•	Records discussing compliance or non-compliance with deed restrictions, federal law, or veteran housing obligations
	•	Environmental, safety, traffic, or land-use reviews related to Brentwood School’s activities
	•	Internal memoranda, approvals, oversight documents, and enforcement evaluations

This request includes records referring to the land or facilities under any name or description, including but not limited to:
	•	“Brentwood School athletic fields”
“Brentwood school”
	•	“Brentwood School lease”
	•	“Brentwood School use agreement”
	•	“West Los Angeles VA Soldiers Home fields”
	•	Any parcel number, map reference, or internal designation associated with Brentwood School use

This request seeks all responsive records in any format, including electronic records, emails, attachments, maps, diagrams, reports, presentations, and memoranda.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

sennett

communications.subject: VHA Fee Estimate FOIA Request: 26-05337-F
communications.communication:
This email seeks confirmation of your willingness to pay the fees associated with your December 26, 2025, Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) requests to the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA). In accordance with 38 C.F.R. §1.561(h)(2), if you do not confirm your willingness to pay the estimated fees within 10 business days, we will administratively close your request. This request has been placed on hold pending your response. The FOIA places all requesters in one of three categories for fee purposes: (1) commercial use requesters; (2) educational institutions; non-commercial, scientific institutions; and representatives of the news media; and (3) all other requesters. We have determined your request to be commercial use because you are requesting commercial records and public records related to these sites are already in the public domain. As a commercial use requester, you will be charged search and review fees in accordance with 38 C.F.R. §1.561. The minimum fee estimate for your request in $250.00, but it may exceed several thousand dollars.  Please contact me if you wish to modify your request to lower the estimated fee. Be advised that the records may be redacted in whole or in part, even though you have paid the search fees to locate those records. In accordance with 38 C.F.R. §1.561(l)(2), we are requesting pre-payment of the estimated fees before we process your request. The actual fees could be higher or lower than the estimate; a refund will be issued if needed. You will receive an email with instructions to pay online. Please notify me via email when the payment is submitted. If you have any questions, please contact me at 802-369-4606 . Sincerely, 
Joseph Smeraldi

files.source: West Los Angeles VA Medical Center, United States of America

communications.subject: RE: Freedom of Information Act Request #26-05337-F
communications.communication:
Dear FOIA Officer,
I am writing in response to your notice assessing a $250 fee and subsequently closing the above referenced request. I respectfully request that this fee assessment be withdrawn and that the request be reopened and processed.
This request qualifies for a fee waiver under the Freedom of Information Act. The records were requested in the interest of the public and for the purpose of informing the public about the operations and activities of a federal agency or public department, not for commercial use. Disclosure of the requested records is in the public interest because it is likely to contribute significantly to public understanding of government operations, decisions, and conduct.
As the requester, I am a journalist and public interest requester using MuckRock to obtain records for public dissemination. The information sought will be analyzed and shared through public reporting and educational platforms. It will not be used for commercial gain. In addition, I lack the financial resources to pay a $250 fee, and imposing such a charge creates a substantial barrier to public access to information that the law is intended to make available to the public.
The estimated $250 fee also appears excessive and unsupported without a detailed breakdown of the anticipated search, review, or duplication costs. FOIA requires agencies to make reasonable efforts to narrow requests, consider partial disclosures, and communicate with requesters before closing a request due to fees. No meaningful attempt was made to contact me to discuss scope reduction or to explain why this fee was necessary.
Closing the request solely due to a fee estimate, without providing an opportunity to discuss scope or apply a fee waiver, is inconsistent with FOIA’s procedural requirements and its purpose of promoting transparency.
For these reasons, I respectfully request that:
The $250 fee assessment be withdrawn or waived;
The request be reopened; and
The records be processed and released as required by law.
If the agency maintains that any fees are unavoidable, please provide a detailed written itemization justifying the charge and identify the specific statutory provisions being relied upon.
Thank you for your attention to this matter. I look forward to your prompt response and to the reopening and fulfillment of this request.
Sincerely,
Sennett Devermont

communications.subject: RE: [EXTERNAL] RE: Freedom of Information Act Request #26-00310-A
communications.communication:
Your appeal is still pending with our office. Given the variant circumstances that affect the length of time required to respond to each appeal, including the number and size of documents requested, the need to retrieve those records, the complexity of the legal issues involved, and the current workload of the assigned attorney, it is difficult to determine precisely when a decision will be rendered in a particular case.

Additionally, during the appeal process we are unable to offer an estimate as to when your appeal will be completed. Please note, this entire process may take several months or more depending on the complexity of your appeal and other mitigating factors.


V/r,







The Office of General Counsel
Information & Administrative Law Group (024)
Department of Veterans Affairs
810 Vermont Avenue, NW
Washington, DC 20420


PRE-DECISIONAL

Teams are not only measured by their success but by their ability to work together. Having a plan for conflict resolution can lessen the obstacles associated with teamwork. Effective decision making skills and coming to a consensus can increase the team’s productivity and efficiency, thus making the quest for the desired outcome easier. Whereas individuals merely rely on themselves, teams have to rely on each other. An ideal working environment, the understanding of assigned roles, mutual respect, focusing on the common goal, and effective communication skills are all important factors in the success of any team.

title: Budget (Richwood Police Department)

communications.subject: West Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: Budget (Richwood Police Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the West Virginia Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

The itemized budget, sometimes called a Form 700, allocated for your department for the years 2020 through 2025, as well as records sufficient to show any additional funding received through other means, including but not limited to:
• funds for capital expenditures
• grants
• donations

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Dillon Bergin

title: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Johnston County Sheriff's Office)

communications.subject: North Carolina Public Records Law Request: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Johnston County Sheriff's Office)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the North Carolina Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

Use of force (UOF) data dating from January 1, 2017 to December 31, 2025. If possible, please provide this data in database or spreadsheet format and include the case number, incident date, incident time, subject injured indicator, subject injury force related info (e.g. Yes or No), subject age, subject gender, subject ethnicity, subject weapon type, incident area occurred, force type, officer division (e.g. law enforcement operations, detention/correctional operations, etc.), badge number and names associated with the officers involved. If one or more of these fields or dates is unavailable, provide all fields and/or information that is available responsive to my request. If no database or spreadsheet format is available, please provide copies of each individual use of force report or other report where use of force information is documented/reported by officers. Note, this is for all use of force incidents reported by your agency, not limited to only those incidents required to be reported under state or federal programs.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Samuel Sinyangwe

title: Intelligence, classification, and public knowledge

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Request: Intelligence, classification, and public knowledge
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

This is a request under the Freedom of Information Act.  I hereby request the following records:

Any analysis conducted within the CIA examining the role of that either (a) excessive classification of documents or (b) secrecy by intelligence agencies, American or foreign, plays in any of the following:

(1) Distorting the public or academic understanding of the historical record
(2) Modifying or subverting democratic institutions, elections, public opinion, and/or democratic decision-making processes
(3) Altering policy-making or the thinking of legislators or other policy-makers

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.


Sincerely,

Ryan Weisensee
A fix is required to perfect the request.

title: Nugan-Hand Bank (CIA)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

This is a request under the Freedom of Information Act.  I hereby request the following records:

Records relating to Nugan-Hand Bank,  an Australian merchant bank that collapsed in 1980 and was allegedly involved in drug smuggling, arranging weapons deals, and providing a front for the United States Central Intelligence Agency. The bank had employed a number of retired United States military and intelligence officers, including former CIA director William Colby.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Michael Best

title: Marines support for 2025 film "Mission: Impossible - The Final Reckoning"

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Marines support for 2025 film "Mission: Impossible - The Final Reckoning"
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

All documents pertaining to collaboration between the US Marine Corps and the production of the 2025 movie "Mission: Impossible - The Final Reckoning," directed by Christopher McQuarrie and starring Tom Cruise. According to a document released to me by the Marines through FOIA (see link below), the Marines provided support to this production. I therefore request any and all documents pertaining to Marine Corps support for "Mission: Impossible - The Final Reckoning." Such documents may include but should not be not limited to: script notes; production assistance agreements; photographs; and all records of communications between employees of the Marines and members of the cast, crew, and production company of "Mission: Impossible - The Final Reckoning," including memos, notes of meetings and conference calls, emails, and other forms of communication.

Here is a link to the aforementioned document:

https://app.box.com/s/t7tbqia68nc136qisb88aw4yvsoajzem

I am the Director of Research Operations at a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization called The Decoding Culture Foundation as well as the editor-in-chief and a contributor to a research magazine published by said foundation titled "Cultural Engineering Studies." These documents will be used for public education and research purposes. I therefore request a waiver of all fees that may otherwise apply. Attached is a document with organization letterhead confirming my position, as well as the organization's 501(c)(3) application and approval form.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Brett Carollo

communications.subject: RE: Freedom of Information Act Request #2026-USMCFOIA-002520
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern,

This message pertains to FOIA request #2026-USMCFOIA-002520.

In your final response, you released a two-page email message to me, heavily redacted. I am writing to appeal some of the redactions, particularly the redacted material following the words "Rewrites of the script now include...." Such information would be undoubtedly of interest to the public, in terms of learning what sorts of changes are brought about in film productions as a result of federal government collaboration. Such information would not be proprietary, insofar as the film is already released.

Very Respectfully,

Brett Carollo
A copy of documents responsive to the request.

title: FOIA Earl Hayes - United States Navy

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: FOIA Earl Hayes - United States Navy
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Official Military Personnel File (OMPF) concerning subject below
Name of Veteran: Earl Glenn Hayes
Branch: United States Navy
DOB: August 17, 1960
Dates of Service: 1978 to 1984
Date of Death: February 13, 1984

please provide the following documents:
1) History of Assignments NAVPERS form 601-5
2) all Navy Occupational training history NAVPERS 601-4 , Cumulative transfers and receipts NAVPERS 601-12, 
3) Navy Unit Commendation citation award
4) list of awards, decorations, Badges
5) Military Education, training courses
6) Dates of service, dates of ranks, campaigns 

Awards include Navy Commendation Medal, Navy Unit Commendation, Navy good conduct medal, Expert Rifle Ribbon. NEC 5326 Combatant Swimmer. 

Obituary
https://www.newspapers.com/article/the-pantagraph-earl-hayes-obituary-1984/7587918/?locale=en-US

First name: Earl
Middle initial: G
Last name: Hayes
DOB 8/17/1960

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Eric Bradley

communications.subject: Fwd: Freedom of Information Act Request: FOIA Earl Hayes - United States Navy
communications.communication:
Dear Mr. Bradley:

Thank you for submitting your Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) request to
the National Archives and Records Administration’s (NARA) Office of General
Counsel Operational FOIA and Access Division via a referral email to
foia@nara.gov. We received your request on February 17, 2026.  Your request
concerns the *Official Military Personnel File (OMPF) of Earl Glenn Hayes.*

Due to the nature of your inquiry, we are forwarding your request by this
email to the National Personnel Records Center (NPRC) for Military Records
in St. Louis for appropriate handling.  The NPRC will assign your request a
tracking number and respond directly to you.  In the future, if you need to
contact the NPRC, you can do so by mail or fax to the following address:

National Personnel Records Center
1 Archives Drive
St. Louis, Missouri 63138

Telephone:   314-801-0800
Telephone (Toll Free):  1-866-272-6272 (only within the US)
Fax:  314-801-9195

For your reference, below you will find your original submission.

On our website at https://www.archives.gov/veterans/military-service-records
you can find information about requesting Official Military Personnel Files
(OMPFs) that are not yet archival records fully open to the public.  At the
website for the National Archives at St. Louis,
https://www.archives.gov/st-louis/ <https://www.archives.gov/st-louis/%20>,
you will find information about requesting Official Military Personnel
Files that are now archival records open to the public.  The OMPF normally
becomes an archival record 62 years after the date of discharge.

At www.archives.gov/foia <http://www.archives.gov/foia%20> we provide
information about FOIA.  On our home page, www.archives.gov, you can learn
about the resources that we offer to the public, and access our online
catalog.

Sincerely,

<https://www.archives.gov/>

  Ashley Bryan

  301-837-3642

title: OSS Records of Dr Rudolph Katz

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: OSS Records of Dr Rudolph Katz
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

OSS Records pertaining to Dr Rudolph Katz, source of several references on Nazi Germany and its leaders produced by the OSS in 1942, as mentioned on multiple pages in CIA reading room document CIA-RDP82-00038R001000150010-5

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Patrick Mockridge

title: 2026 Brady List (Whatcom County Prosecuting Attorney)

communications.subject: Washington Public Records Act Request: 2026 Brady List (Whatcom County Prosecuting Attorney)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

All current "Brady lists" and "Giglio lists" (per Brady v. Maryland/Giglio v. United States) maintained by your office, along with accompanying potential impeachment material.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Rose Terse

communications.subject: Public Records Request :: R000017-010526
communications.communication:
Dear Rose Terse: 
Thank you for your interest in public records of Whatcom County. Your request has been received and is being processed in accordance with the State of Washington Public Records Act, Chapter 42.56 RCW. Your request was received in this office on 1/5/2026 and given the reference number R000017-010526 for tracking purposes. Records Requested: To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records: 

All current "Brady lists" and "Giglio lists" (per Brady v. Maryland/Giglio v. United States) maintained by your office, along with accompanying potential impeachment material. 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Rose Terse 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2169962/ 
Not all public documents are available in electronic format. If the document(s) requested are not available electronically, we will make them available for inspection or by paper copy in accordance with the Public Records Act, Chapter 42.56 RCW. 
 
Your request will be forwarded to the relevant department(s) to locate the information you seek and to determine the volume and any costs associated with satisfying your request. You will be contacted about the availability and/or provided with copies of the records in question. PLEASE NOTE: The State of Washington Public Records Act, Chapter 42.56 RCW, does not require a governmental body to create new information, to do legal research, or to answer questions. 
You can monitor the progress of your request at the link below and you'll receive an email when your request has been completed. Again, thank you for using the Public Records Center. 
Whatcom County Public Records Officer

communications.subject: Whatcom County Public Records Request - R000017-010526
communications.communication:
Good afternoon,

I'm in receipt of your request to resend the Brady list and accompanying PID materials for Whatcom County.  I am preparing a response now, and hope to have that out to you today.

The second part of your request asks for "accompanying potential impeachment material."  Will the accompanying PID letters with a brief explanation of the reasons for for addition to the list be sufficient?

My direct line is 360-778-5755 if you would like to discuss.

Best,


Megan K. Winter, Paralegal- Civil Division
Whatcom County Prosecuting Attorney
311 Grand Avenue, Suite 201
Bellingham, WA 98225
(360) 778-5710
MWinter@co.whatcom.wa.us<mailto:MWinter@co.whatcom.wa.us>

Please be advised that these communications may be subject to disclosure under RCW 42.56, the Washington State Public Records Act.

CONFIDENTIAL OR PRIVILEGED:  This communication is intended only for the use of the individuals to whom it is addressed and may contain information that is privileged, confidential or exempt from other disclosure under applicable law.  If you are not the intended recipient, you are notified that any disclosure, printing, copying, distribution or use of the contents is prohibited.  If you have received this in error, please notify the sender immediately by telephone or by returning it by reply e-mail and then permanently deleting the communication from your system. Thank you.

With the following attachments we consider this request for:
"All current "Brady lists" and "Giglio lists" (per Brady v. Maryland/Giglio v. United States) maintained by your office, along with accompanying potential impeachment material" closed.  

We have provided you with responsive records in our possession.  Because we do not intend to further address this request, the one-year statute of limitations for legal action has begun.  
Please feel free to reach out to the Whatcom County Public Records Officer @ PublicRecordsOfficer@co.whatcom.wa.us, if you have any questions or concerns.

title: Annual NCAA MFRS FY25 Report (Austin Peay State University)

communications.subject: Tennessee Public Records Act Request: Annual NCAA MFRS FY25 Report (Austin Peay State University)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Tennessee Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Greetings,

I am looking to make a request for records related to your athletic department.

1) I would like a copy of the annual NCAA MFRS report for FY2017 - FY2025. These annual reports are submitted by the athletic department and record the revenue/expenses of the department. It is an approximately 90 page document listing revenue and expenses in the athletic department by team. It is a standard annual NCAA form. The latest FY2025 report was due, 1/15/2025 so should be available. If you are unable to include this, can you please specify if you will send it to me immediately when it's available or if you want me to make a new request. That should be 9 reports combined.

2) I'd like copies of the latest contracts for your athletic director and head coaches of football, mens/womens basketball, and mens/womens ice hockey if you have those teams. That should be 6 documents if you have that many teams.

Please let me know if you have any questions. Thank you for your time.

Best,

Greg Chick

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 7 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Kent Hoover, a citizen of Tennessee, in coordination with Greg Chick.

title: EscambiaVotes GIS Mapping Data & IT Administration (2022-Present)

communications.subject: Florida Sunshine Law Request: EscambiaVotes GIS Mapping Data & IT Administration (2022-Present)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Florida Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records:

To the Public Records Custodian / Hon. Robert Bender, Escambia County Supervisor of Elections:

Under the Florida Sunshine Law (Chapter 119, F.S.), I am requesting access to and copies of the following public records regarding the public-facing election results portal hosted on or linked via EscambiaVotes.gov.

Publicly available data on the EscambiaVotes portal indicates a systemic, multi-year degradation of the GIS precinct mapping module. Specifically, the public map functioned correctly during the 2022 General Election (last updated 12/8/2022). However, it suffered a complete failure during the 2024 Primary and General Elections (displaying no map data), and remains only partially functional as of the 2025 Special Election (displaying broken or unlinked shapefiles).

Because the underlying tabular voting data is actively present on the site, this request seeks to audit the administrative failure of the GIS mapping API and the visual transparency tool itself. I am requesting the following records:

1. IT and Administrative Correspondence (Dec 2022 - Present):
Any internal IT helpdesk tickets, error logs, or administrative memorandums generated between December 8, 2022, and the present date regarding GIS shapefile errors, precinct redistricting desynchronization, or the failure of the mapping API to visually populate precinct data on the public portal.

2. Third-Party Vendor Communications:
Any communications (including emails, letters, or service requests) between the Escambia County Supervisor of Elections office and the third-party election software vendor/portal host (e.g., VR Systems) dated between December 8, 2022, and the present date regarding the broken map data, API error resolution, or portal maintenance for the 2024 and 2025 elections.

Statutory Compliance:
If you determine that any portion of these requested records is exempt from disclosure under Chapter 119, Florida law requires you to state in writing the basis for the exemption, including the exact statutory citation authorizing the redaction or denial.

Please advise me of any costs associated with fulfilling this request prior to processing.

Thank you for your lawful compliance.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Matthew Joseph Luecht

communications.subject: RE: Florida Sunshine Law Request: EscambiaVotes GIS Mapping Data & IT Administration (2022-Present)
communications.communication:
To the Public Records Custodian:

On March 31, 2026, a highly specific public records request was submitted to your office pursuant to the Florida Sunshine Law (Chapter 119, F.S.). As of today, April 9, 2026, I have received no acknowledgment of receipt, no status update, and no indication of any costs associated with fulfilling this request.

While Chapter 119 permits agencies a "reasonable time" to compile records, the complete failure to even acknowledge a legally submitted request after seven business days falls outside the statutory expectation of a good-faith response.

If your office intends to claim that the requested administrative records, IT logs, or vendor communications are exempt from public disclosure, Florida law requires you to state that exemption in writing, including the specific statutory citation.

Please immediately confirm receipt of the original March 31 request and provide an estimated timeline for the production of the records or the statutory basis for their denial. Continued silence will be documented as a refusal to comply with Chapter 119.

communications.subject: RE: [EXTERNAL] RE: Florida Sunshine Law Request: EscambiaVotes GIS Mapping Data & IT Administration (2022-Present)
communications.communication:
Mr. Luecht,

Good morning~ After searching our emails, your email below dated April 9, 2026 is the first email we have received from you. When our office receives a public records request, we always acknowledge that we received it.

Please know that we process our public records requests on a first-in, first-out basis. If additional information is needed to fulfill your request, someone from our office will contact you.
If you have any questions, please call or email me. Have a wonderful day!

Angela Crawley
Escambia County Supervisor of Elections Office
213 Palafox Place, 2nd Floor
Pensacola, Florida 32502
(850) 595-3900

communications.subject: RE: Florida Sunshine Law Request #26-32
communications.communication:
Dear Ms. Crawley,

Thank you for your response on April 23, 2026, and for providing the email record from VR Systems.

The document provided is an automated receipt confirming the creation of VR Systems support Ticket #: 53238. While I understand Mr. Grimes may not have initiated further outbound emails to the vendor, the creation of this ticket strongly indicates that additional records regarding the diagnosis and resolution of this issue exist within the agency's possession, whether generated internally or supplied by the vendor.

To ensure full compliance with Chapter 119, Florida Statutes, please consider this a follow-up public records request. I am requesting to inspect or obtain copies of the following:


Ticket Resolution Records: Any and all subsequent emails, vendor portal logs, status updates, or closing notes provided by VR Systems to Escambia County regarding the diagnosis, troubleshooting, and resolution of Ticket #: 53238.


Internal Communications: All internal emails, memorandums, or messages (including text messages or digital chat platforms like MS Teams) sent or received by Rusty Grimes or other SOE staff between May 6, 2025, and May 14, 2025, that reference the precinct mapping issue, the display of election results, or VR Systems.


Incident Documentation: Any IT incident reports, summaries, or documentation detailing the root cause of the precincts failing to display election results and the steps taken to restore the system.

As a reminder, under Florida law, the definition of a public record encompasses all materials made or received by an agency in connection with official business, regardless of the physical form or means of transmission.

If any part of this request is denied, or if any records are redacted, please explicitly cite the statutory exemption authorizing the withholding of the information.

Thank you for your time and your continued assistance with this matter.

title: FWS Space X Plover

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: FWS Space X Plover
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

A copy of the "2025 plover mutlivariable stats analysis", as referenced in the attached document from USFWS Region 4 staff. I also seek all USFWS reports from years 2024 and 2025 concerning the impacts to plovers from SpaceX operations in Florida, including reports referencing take, habitat degradation, population trends, etc. 

I am a reporter for Public Domain and other outlets.  I seek a fee waiver but will pay $25 if that waiver is not forthcoming.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jimmy Tobias

communications.subject: FOIA Request Acknowledgement: DOI-2025-009451
communications.communication:
The United States Fish and Wildlife Service (FWS) FOIA office received your Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) request dated September 11, 2025, and assigned it control number DOI-2025-009451. Please cite this number in any future communications with our office regarding your request for:

A copy of the "2025 plover mutlivariable stats analysis", as referenced in the attached document from USFWS Region 4 staff. I also seek all USFWS reports from years 2024 and 2025 concerning the impacts to plovers from SpaceX operations in Florida, including reports referencing take, habitat degradation, population trends, etc.

The FOIA provides for the collection of fees based on the costs of processing a FOIA request and your fee category. We have classified you as a representative of the news media and you have asked us to waive the fees for processing of your request. In this case, we have determined that there is no billable cost to process your request, therefore, there is no need to address your fee waiver request.

We use Multitrack Processing to process FOIA requests. The Simple track is for requests that would generally take one to five workdays to process. The Normal track is for requests that would generally take six to twenty workdays to process. The Complex track is for requests that would generally take twenty-one to sixty workdays to process. The Extraordinary track is for requests that would generally take more than sixty workdays to process. The Expedited track is for requests that have been granted expedited processing, which are processed as soon as practicable. Within each track, requests are usually processed on a first-in, first-out basis. Your request falls into the Normal processing track. There are 198 requests pending ahead of yours in this processing track. Presuming that our current FOIA capacity and burden remain constant, we expect that we will dispatch a determination to you by October 9, 2025.

If you have any questions, please feel free to contact us at fwhq_foia@fws.gov<mailto:fwhq_foia@fws.gov>.

Sincerely,

Matthew Sullivan
Government Information Specialist
U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service

communications.subject: FOIA Request Acknowledgement: DOI-2025-009451
communications.communication:
The United States Fish and Wildlife Service (FWS) FOIA office received your Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) request dated September 12, 2025, and assigned it control number DOI-2025-009478. Please cite this number in any future communications with our office regarding your request for:

All emails sent or received by FWS biologist Sarah Termondt that contain the following keyword: "SpaceX". This request seeks records produced between May 1, 2024 and May 1, 2025.

The FOIA provides for the collection of fees based on the costs of processing a FOIA request and your fee category. We have classified you as a representative of the news media and you have asked us to waive the fees for processing your request. In this case, we have determined that there is no billable cost to process your request, therefore, there is no need to address your fee waiver request.

We use Multitrack Processing to process FOIA requests. The Simple track is for requests that would generally take one to five workdays to process. The Normal track is for requests that would generally take six to twenty workdays to process. The Complex track is for requests that would generally take twenty-one to sixty workdays to process. The Extraordinary track is for requests that would generally take more than sixty workdays to process. The Expedited track is for requests that have been granted expedited processing, which are processed as soon as practicable. Within each track, requests are usually processed on a first-in, first-out basis. Your request falls into the Normal processing track. There are 198 requests pending ahead of yours in this processing track. Presuming that our current FOIA capacity and burden remain constant, we expect that we will dispatch a determination to you by October 9, 2025.

We use Multitrack Processing to process FOIA requests. The Simple track is for requests that would generally take one to five workdays to process. The Normal track is for requests that would generally take six to twenty workdays to process. The Complex track is for requests that would generally take twenty-one to sixty workdays to process. The Extraordinary track is for requests that would generally take more than sixty workdays to process. The Expedited track is for requests that have been granted expedited processing, which are processed as soon as practicable. Within each track, requests are usually processed on a first-in, first-out basis. Because we will need to search for and collect requested records from field facilities or other units that are separate from the office processing the request, we have determined that your request falls into the Complex processing track and are taking a 10-workday extension under 43 C.F.R. § 2.19(b)<http://www.ecfr.gov/cgi-bin/text-idx?c=ecfr&sid=0fc3ab3499768eebc2e3691c8cf88dec&rgn=div5&view=text&node=43:1.1.1.1.2&idno=43#43:1.1.1.1.2.4.5.6>. There are 452 requests pending ahead of yours in this processing track. Presuming that our current FOIA capacity and burden remain constant, we expect that we will dispatch a determination to you by May 21, 2026.
We apologize for this delay in finalizing our decision. You may narrow the scope of your request to obtain quicker processing in your currently assigned track or move the request into a different track (which may have the effect of having it processed more quickly). You also may agree to an alternative time period for processing by communicating with us. If after contacting us at fwhq_foia@fws.gov,<mailto:fwhq_foia@fws.gov,> you need further information or assistance with your request, you may wish to seek dispute resolution services from the Department’s FOIA Public Liaison by email at doifoiapublicliaison@sol.doi.gov. <mailto:doifoiapublicliaison@sol.doi.gov.>

If you need further information or assistance after contacting the Department’s FOIA Public Liaison, you may wish to seek dispute resolution services from the Office of Government Information Services (OGIS). The 2007 FOIA amendments created the OGIS to offer mediation services to resolve disputes between FOIA requesters and Federal agencies as a non-exclusive alternative to litigation. Using OGIS services does not affect your right to pursue litigation. You may contact OGIS in any of the following ways:

Office of Government Information Services
National Archives and Records Administration
8601 Adelphi Road - OGIS
College Park, MD 20740-6001
E-mail: ogis@nara.gov<mailto:ogis@nara.gov>
Web: https://www.archives.gov/ogis
Telephone: 202-741-5770
Toll-free: 1-877-684-6448

Please note that using OGIS services does not affect the timing of filing an appeal with the Department’s FOIA & Privacy Act Appeals Officer.

You may file an appeal to the Department’s FOIA/Privacy Act Appeals Officer regarding this response. If you choose to appeal, the FOIA/Privacy Act Appeals Officer must receive your FOIA appeal no later than 90 workdays from the date of this communication. Appeals arriving or delivered after 5 p.m. Eastern Time, Monday through Friday, will be deemed received on the next workday.
Your appeal must be made in writing. You may submit your appeal and accompanying materials to the FOIA/Privacy Act Appeals Officer by mail, courier service, fax, or email. All communications concerning your appeal should be clearly marked with the words: "FREEDOM OF INFORMATION APPEAL." You must include an explanation of why you believe this response is in error. You must also include with your appeal copies of all correspondence between you and FWS concerning your FOIA request, including your original FOIA request and this response. Failure to include with your appeal all correspondence between you and FWS will result in the Department's rejection of your appeal, unless the FOIA/Privacy Act Appeals Officer determines (in the FOIA/Privacy Act Appeals Officer’s sole discretion) that good cause exists to accept the defective appeal.
Please include your name and daytime telephone number (or the name and telephone number of an appropriate contact), email address and fax number (if available) in case the FOIA/Privacy Act Appeals Officer needs additional information or clarification of your appeal.

DOI FOIA/Privacy Act Appeals Office Contact Information
Department of the Interior
Office of the Solicitor
1849 C Street, N.W.
MS-6556 MIB
Washington, DC 20240
Attn: FOIA/Privacy Act Appeals Office
Telephone: (202) 208-5339
Fax: (202) 208-6677
Email: FOIA.Appeals@sol.doi.gov<mailto:FOIA.Appeals@sol.doi.gov>

If you have any questions, please feel free to contact us at fwhq_foia@fws.gov<mailto:fwhq_foia@fws.gov>.

Sincerely,

Matthew Sullivan
Government Information Specialist
U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service
